title: Independent Semantic, Investigative, and Calculus Variation Benchmark
purpose: Test whether candidate vocabularies preserve independent variation among representation, interpretation, investigation, and reasoning calculus rather than merely simulating one execution trace.
claim_boundary: A result applies only to the frozen matched cases and candidate vocabularies tested. It does not establish universal necessity, global minimality, or universal reasoning coverage.

system_a.investigation: determine whether the alarm is permitted
calculus: from p and p_implies_q derive q
from p and p_implies_q derive q

matched_cases.required_discrimination: The mapping must preserve that the same syntax and inference pattern carry different denotations and information content.

system_b.investigation: determine whether the fact set is internally consistent

matched_cases.required_discrimination: The mapping must preserve different objectives and success conditions over the same represented and interpreted material.
The mapping must preserve that different admissibility rules produce different permitted conclusions under the same objective.
The mapping must preserve distinct representational organization without treating shared interpreted content as representational identity.

evaluation_rules.hidden_reintroduction_test: commitment-equivalent recovered machinery counts as the removed primitive

success_conditions.existential_sufficiency: at least one of three mappings passes all six preservation dimensions for every matched case
success_conditions.reproducible_sufficiency: all three mappings pass all six preservation dimensions for every matched case
success_conditions.merger_failure: a merged vocabulary cannot discriminate the changed commitment without hidden reintroduction or preservation loss
